item.title: RFK Jr.’s New Normal
item.description: As Trump nominates a new, uncontroversial CDC director, a more restrained version of the health secretary is appearing.
item.dc:creator: Tom Bartlett

item.title: Israel Moderates Are Losing the Democratic Party
item.description: Their position has become untenable. But liberal Zionists can adapt.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Chait

item.title: Today’s Atlantic Trivia: Name the Pol, Then the Actor
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: The Tyranny of AI Everywhere
item.description: Sneakers? Why stop there?
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Petri

item.title: Trump Voters Are Over It
item.description: A shocking number of the president’s supporters have turned against him.
item.dc:creator: Yvonne Wingett Sanchez

item.title: What I Saw Inside the Kennedy Center
item.description: I spent 10 months working at the institution because I thought I could help protect it. What I observed there is far worse than the public knows.
item.dc:creator: Josef Palermo

item.title: The Quiet Way Authoritarianism Begins to Crumble
item.description: Among the many reasons for Viktor Orbán’s defeat was the rural clubs where citizens relearned democratic habits.
item.dc:creator: Gal Beckerman

item.title: The DNA Fix for Aging
item.description: Everyone’s DNA keeps mutating. Could correcting those errors lead to longevity?
item.dc:creator: Roxanne Khamsi

item.title: AI’s Next Frontier: People Skills
item.description: Imagine a chatbot that actually knows how to talk to you.
item.dc:creator: Matteo Wong

item.title: Pope James David Vance the First
item.description: The vice president has decided he’s a more accomplished theologian than Leo XIV.
item.dc:creator: Tom Nichols

item.title: If You Want a Better World, Act Like You Live in It
item.description: We’ve had Henry David Thoreau the environmentalist, the libertarian, the life coach. To understand his influence, think of him first as a dissident.
item.dc:creator: Gal Beckerman

item.title: If Hungary Can Do It
item.description: Viktor Orbán offered a model for antidemocratic rule, one admired by Donald Trump and other world leaders. What does his stunning loss after 16 years in power mean?
item.dc:creator: Hanna Rosin

item.title: A Pillar of the Economics Establishment Admits That It Was Wrong
item.description: In a new report, the World Bank thinks better of its old free-market absolutism.
item.dc:creator: Rogé Karma

item.title: History Is Running Backwards
item.description: Why reactionaries are taking over the world
item.dc:creator: David Brooks

item.title: Trump Doesn’t Have the Power to Enact His Latest Elections Scheme
item.description: The president’s attempts to interfere with the midterms demand vigilance, but a recent flimsy gambit is an argument against despair.
item.dc:creator: David A. Graham

item.title: It’s Not Just Iran. Trump Is Flailing on Multiple Fronts.
item.description: The president is on a losing streak, and even some of his aides are dismayed by his choices.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lemire

item.title: The Publishing Mystery That No One Wants to Talk About
item.description: A minimally speaking autistic man just wrote a best-selling book. Or did he?
item.dc:creator: Daniel Engber

item.title: Is Anybody Actually Winning Trump’s Iran War?
item.description: Former U.S. Deputy National Security Adviser Matt Pottinger on the U.S.-Iran cease-fire, Trump’s Hormuz blockade, and China’s reaction to the Iran war. Plus: A seismic election in Hungary, andLabyrinths, by Jorge Luis Borges.
item.dc:creator: David Frum

item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: Tax Day
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: Jared Kushner’s Mysterious Role in the Trump Administration
item.description: Is the president’s son-in-law carrying out the public’s business or pursuing his own private interests?
item.dc:creator: Andrea Bernstein
